preFix.title: Replacing seat foam in an armchair
preFix.subtitle: Cut and fit new high-density foam in a drop-in or sprung seat
preFix.excerpt: How to replace sagging or compressed seat foam in an armchair: removing the old foam and cambric base, cutting a replacement from sheet foam, wrapping it with Dacron to give a rounded edge profile, and refitting the cover.
preFix.sourceNotes: Practical Upholstery (C.H. Doust). Collins Complete DIY Manual. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: A seat that has become permanently compressed rarely needs a full reupholster; replacing the foam is often sufficient to restore firmness and shape.
is available from upholstery suppliers cut to any size. The Dacron wrap that goes over the foam softens the edge profile and prevents the fabric from wearing through on the foam corner.

High resilience seat foam in the appropriate thickness (50 to 75 mm for a traditional chair seat, 100 mm for a deeper cushion). Dacron wrap, 50 mm wider than the foam on all sides. Spray contact adhesive for bonding the Dacron. A long-bladed upholstery or carving knife, or an electric carving knife, for cutting foam cleanly. Staple remover, staple gun, and 14 mm staples.

Remove the seat cover by pulling out the staples with the staple remover, working around the perimeter. Lift off the cover and set it aside; do not cut it unless you intend to replace the fabric. Remove the old foam: if it is glued to a platform seat or webbing, peel it away. If there is a
base cloth, remove and replace it if it is torn.
Step 2, cut and wrap the new foam

Cut the new foam to the exact plan size of the seat platform using a long-bladed knife with a smooth sawing action; do not press down on the foam as this causes a bowed cut. Spray contact adhesive on the foam top and around the edges, and on the inside face of the Dacron, then press the Dacron onto the foam, folding the excess over the bottom edge. The Dacron softens the corners so the cover fabric does not pull tight over a sharp foam edge.

Place the foam on the seat platform, then lay the original cover over it face down. Pull the cover taut at the front, staple the front edge to the underside of the seat rail, then repeat at the back and both sides. Work from the centre of each side outward to keep the cover grain straight. Fold and staple the corners cleanly, trimming away excess fabric bulk before the final staple at each corner.
